why: Protocol 775 Login serverbound minecraft:custom_query_answer must dispatch from the official framed packet id to Stevenarella's Login serverbound decoder through packet::packet_by_id and consume the official transaction id plus nullable payload marker body bytes. This is packet framing/dispatch/decode evidence only and is separate from plugin channel handling, custom payload semantics, login acknowledgement, Configuration entry, or client-load completion.
what: The Rust oracle test login_custom_query_answer_serverbound_framed_dispatch_matches_official_oracle_answer panics while exercising packet::packet_by_id(775, State::Login, Direction::Serverbound, packet id 2, official custom_query_answer body bytes). Protocol 775 Login serverbound currently maps minecraft:hello / 0x00 and minecraft:key / 0x01, so the official minecraft:custom_query_answer / 0x02 table row is unsupported by the active version map.

evidence.summary: Generated from official client.jar direct calls: ServerboundCustomQueryAnswerPacket constructor and STREAM_CODEC, LoginProtocols.SERVERBOUND.codec().encode/decode(ServerboundCustomQueryAnswerPacket), LoginProtocols.SERVERBOUND_TEMPLATE.details().listPackets(...), and official bytecode fields transactionId/payload/readPayload/write.
Manifest declares the exact Rust oracle test and framed_dispatch_decode comparison surface.
Rust test reads the manifest and answer, splits official framed VarInt packet id from body bytes, and calls packet::packet_by_id for Login serverbound custom_query_answer dispatch.
Current Protocol 775 Login serverbound table contains minecraft:hello / 0x00 and minecraft:key / 0x01 only and has no mapping for official packet id 0x02.

observed_failure: thread 'login_custom_query_answer_oracle' panicked at stevenarella/protocol/src/protocol/versions/v26_1_2.rs:1:1: bad packet id 0x2 in Serverbound Login; Stevenarella panicked while dispatching official Login serverbound custom_query_answer packet id 2
